summary: Reframes innovation support focus to include SMCs and SMEs, including start-ups

Limits applicability for high-risk AI in products covered by Annex I Section B harmonisation legislation to Article 6(1), Article 60a, Articles 102-109, 111, 112 only; Article 57 applies only insofar as requirements have been integrated
Adds definitions for 'SME' (per Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC) and 'SMC' (per Commission Recommendation 2025/1099)
AI literacy obligation changed from mandatory to 'encourage' — Commission and Member States shall encourage providers and deployers; training obligations for high-risk deployers remain
Providers who consider Annex III AI system is not high-risk must document assessment and provide to national authorities on request
Para 1: references new Article 4a(1) for data quality; Para 5: deleted; Para 6: references Article 4a(1) for non-training-technique systems
SMCs and SMEs (including start-ups) may use simplified technical documentation form established by Commission
QMS implementation proportionate to provider size, especially SMCs/SMEs including start-ups
Single application and assessment procedure for notifying authorities under both AI Act and Union harmonisation legislation in Annex I Section A
Notified bodies designated under other harmonisation legislation may use documents/certificates to support AI Act designation; single assessment procedure for Annex I Section A bodies
Notification based on codes/categories/types in new Annex XIV; Commission delegated acts power to amend Annex XIV

High-risk AI systems covered by Annex I Section A follow relevant harmonisation legislation conformity assessment; notified bodies under that legislation can assess; transition period 18 months; dual-classification systems follow harmonisation legislation procedure
Registration obligation for Article 6(3) AI systems in EU database removed
AI Office codes of practice for transparency obligations; Commission may assess adequacy and adopt implementing act if inadequate
Commission publishes assessment of codes of practice adequacy
AI Office may establish EU-level sandbox for Article 75(1) AI systems; sandbox plans incorporate real-world testing in single document
Commission implementing acts for detailed sandbox arrangements including governance, coordination, common principles
Real-world testing scope extended to Annex I Section A covered systems; broader testing by providers/prospective providers
Simplified QMS compliance extended from microenterprises to all SMEs including start-ups
Scientific panel fee structure simplified; Member States can consult experts directly without Commission involvement
National competent authorities guidance to SMCs/SMEs/start-ups; consultation with other competent authorities on cross-domain matters
Post-market monitoring plan part of technical documentation; Commission to adopt guidance (not harmonised template)

Title changed to 'Market surveillance and control of AI systems and mutual assistance'; AI Office exclusively competent for GPAI-based AI systems on same-provider basis and VLOP/VLOSE systems; enforcement powers via implementing acts; procedural safeguards (Reg 2019/1020 Art 18 mutatis mutandis); pre-market conformity assessments by Commission

Title changed to 'Powers of authorities protecting fundamental rights and cooperation with market surveillance authorities'; scope clarified; mutual assistance obligation added between market surveillance and fundamental rights authorities
Codes of conduct to account for SMC/SME/start-up needs
Commission guidelines to pay particular attention to SMC/SME/start-up needs
Penalties may include warnings and non-monetary measures; SMC/SME fines capped at lower of percentage or amount thresholds
Grace period for existing high-risk systems clarified (type and model basis); new transitional period for Art 50(2) marking obligations (6 months for systems placed on market before 2 Aug 2026)

Chapter III application linked to Commission decision confirming adequate compliance measures; fallback dates: 2 Dec 2027 for Art 6(2)/Annex III, 2 Aug 2028 for Art 6(1)/Annex I; Articles 102-110 apply from entry into application of this amending Regulation
